translation_text: In the highest antiquity, (the people) did not know that there were (their rulers).
translation_text_raw: In the highest antiquity, (the people) did not know that there were (their rulers).

translation_text: In the next age they loved them and praised them. In the next they feared them;
translation_text_raw: In the next age they loved them and praised them. In the next they feared them;

chinese_text: 其次畏之，其次侮之。信不足焉，
translation_text: in the next they despised them. Thus it was that when faith (in the Dao) was deficient (in the rulers) a want of faith in them ensued (in the people).
translation_text_raw: in the next they despised them. Thus it was that when faith (in the Dao) was deficient (in the rulers) a want of faith in them ensued (in the people).

translation_text: How irresolute did those (earliest rulers) appear, showing (by their reticence) the importance which they set upon their words!
translation_text_raw: How irresolute did those (earliest rulers) appear, showing (by their reticence) the importance which they set upon their words!

chinese_text: 功成事遂，百姓皆謂我自然。
translation_text: Their work was done and their undertakings were successful, while the people all said, 'We are as we are, of ourselves!'
translation_text_raw: Their work was done and their undertakings were successful, while the people all said, 'We are as we are, of ourselves!'
